evals.prompt: I need to design a URL shortener service. It should handle ~10M shortened URLs, support custom slugs, and track click analytics. Help me design the architecture.
evals.expected_output: A structured architecture document with at minimum: system context or container Mermaid diagram, components table, key design decisions, and a scalability/bottlenecks section that proactively identifies hot-read bottlenecks, database write throughput, and analytics pipeline concerns.
expectations: Output includes at least one Mermaid diagram
Output has a Scalability & Bottlenecks section with a table
Output identifies the read-heavy redirect workload as a bottleneck (and suggests caching)
Output has a Components table listing distinct components
Output documents at least one key design decision with rationale

evals.prompt: We're deciding between PostgreSQL and MongoDB for a new user-profile service. Most data is structured but we need flexible attributes per user. Write an ADR.
evals.expected_output: A complete ADR following the standard format: Status, Date, Deciders, Context, Options Considered (with pros/cons for each), Decision with specific rationale, and Consequences (positive, negative, neutral). The decision rationale should be concrete, not generic.
expectations: Output follows ADR format with Status, Date, Context, Options Considered, Decision, and Consequences sections
Output lists at least 2 options with explicit pros and cons for each
The Decision section gives specific rationale (not just 'it is better')
The Consequences section includes at least one negative trade-off

evals.prompt: How should I structure a notification service that sends emails, SMS, and push notifications? We currently have a monolith but want to extract this as a separate service.
evals.expected_output: Architecture guidance covering: the service boundary and how the monolith will communicate with the notification service (sync vs async), channel abstraction (email/SMS/push as pluggable providers), a Mermaid diagram showing the architecture, and a bottlenecks section that addresses queue depth, provider rate limits, and retry/failure handling.
expectations: Output includes at least one Mermaid diagram showing the notification service architecture
Output addresses the monolith extraction concern (how existing monolith calls the new service)
Output has a Scalability & Bottlenecks section
Output identifies external provider rate limits as a bottleneck
Output recommends async messaging (queue/event-based) for the monolith-to-service communication
